purpose: Verify copied artifacts against checksum manifests to detect data corruption or tampering in backup, deployment, and migration workflows.

endpoints.description: Check status of a running verification task.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Verify copied artifacts against checksum manifests to detect corruption or tampering.

agent_usage.when_to_use: When you need to verify that files were copied or transferred without corruption. Ideal after backup operations, deployment pushes, or data migrations.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: When you need semantic content validation, format conversion verification, or comparison of file meaning rather than byte-level integrity.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Generate checksum manifest with the module. 2) Copy artifacts to destination. 3) Call /verify with the manifest to validate.

failure_modes.condition: Manifest file not found or unreadable
failure_modes.recovery: Ensure manifest path is correct and file is readable

failure_modes.symptom: failure entry in result listing missing file path
failure_modes.recovery: Restore or re-provision the missing artifact

forbidden_behaviors.description: Do not modify source artifacts being verified
Do not perform semantic content validation
Do not require external network access

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Use Node crypto module to compute checksums. Keep API small and deterministic. Support streaming verification for large files.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Large manifests may cause memory pressure — consider streaming the manifest parse.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Streaming checksum computation with async generators
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Loading entire files into memory before hashing
